author: @townbell2248
text: Did that have a square capacitor on it?  Mine has one it. It has 2 fans with the motor inverted them with the capacitor connected by 2 wires. There is no exposed metal to short the capacitor out. Im really frustrated and want to take the fans off but not sure how to mess with the capacitor 😢

author: @michaelmoloney4080
text: He does a few videos a while back where he wires steppers alternators and PC fans into generators, browsing his older videos is rewarding, also instead of adding a stepper at the other end, do some gearing to get more speed or torque...also has a few videos about that. And a video how to just build a generator 😂 honestly this is great prepper stuff

I connected it to a phone charger plug, it just keeps bleeping and saying charging on and off but it's actually running the battery down, any ideas?

author: @Nuovoswiss
text:
I would love to see a follow-up on this. This type of fan is entirely dependent on its housing to determine what direction it sends air in (the rotor is axially symmetric), so a generator should be as well. There's a good youtube video illustrating this titled "Tower fans are kind of weird".
There was also a related AskEngineers thread titled "Trying to understand how airflow works in a tower fan", where there was some more speculation about the optimal housing shape. That may also have implications for a generator.

author: @bartronicsecurity
text: As a young man I learnt a very important piece of advice from a wise old man who became a mentor to me. Everything we do in life should be looked at from a point of ROI in time or cost or both. We don't live for ever and we don't have infinite resources so don't waste either.  I have lived by that mantra for over 35 years now. And this is yet another typical good ROI project. Nice one.

author: @hrothgeirrH

author: @davidl.howser9707
text: Robert, Do you have a local electronics recycler, or government electronics drop off recycling center in your town? Maybe abundant cross flow fans can be found there. Unfortunately our local recycle center only allows drop off of electronics, and strictly enforces no scavenging take home electronics bits. I have had much luck on facebook requesting of members in my area, if they have fans lying about that they wish to donate free of charge. They leave the fans out in a trash bag, or set on a porch for me to stop by, and pick up. Thank you for your most educational efforts, and fun to watch mannerisms. Enjoy your time thinking and tinkering.
